name: Neural Tangents (Infinite-Width Neural Network Kernels)

performance.notes: JAX JIT + GPU; kernel matrix computation O(n^2) in dataset size

agent_readiness.notes: Infinite-width neural network theory (NTK/NNGP) for JAX. import neural_tangents as nt. from neural_tangents import stax. init_fn, apply_fn, kernel_fn = stax.serial(stax.Dense(512), stax.Relu(), stax.Dense(1)). kernel = kernel_fn(X, X2, 'ntk'). predict_fn = nt.predict.gradient_descent_mse_ensemble(kernel_fn, X_train, Y_train). Y_pred = predict_fn(t=None, x_test=X_test, get='ntk'). Research tool for neural network theory. NNGP: Bayesian NN in infinite-width limit. NTK: lazy training regime.

gotchas: Highly specialized — only for neural network theory research (NTK/NNGP)
Requires understanding of infinite-width neural networks and kernel methods
Kernel matrix O(n^2) — impractical for large datasets
stax API (functional JAX NN) differs from modern Flax/Haiku
best_when: Neural Tangent Kernel (NTK) and NNGP research
Studying neural network training dynamics in the infinite-width limit
Theoretical comparison of finite vs infinite width neural networks
Gaussian process prediction via NNGP posterior
avoid_when: Large datasets where kernel matrix is intractable
Without theoretical background in NTK theory
summary: Neural Tangents implements infinite-width neural network kernels (NTK/NNGP) in JAX. stax.serial(Dense(512), Relu(), Dense(1)). kernel_fn(X, X2, 'ntk'). Bayesian GP prediction via NNGP. NTK for lazy training regime analysis. Apache-2.0 by Google Brain. Pure research tool for neural network theory. Not for practical deep learning. Kernel matrix is O(n^2) — small datasets only.
